subject: Mathematics
title: Greatest Common Divisor
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Greatest Common Divisor.

hook.text: Two numbers always share at least one common factor: the number 1.

buildup.text: The greatest common divisor of 12 and 18 is 6. It's the largest number dividing both.

discovery.text: Euclid's algorithm finds the GCD by repeatedly dividing and taking remainders.

twist.text: This algorithm is over 2,300 years old and remains the fastest known method.

climax.text: GCDs simplify fractions, sync gears in machines, and underpin modern cryptography.

punchline.text: The oldest algorithm ever recorded is still unbeaten.

quiz.question: What is the GCD of 12 and 18?
